title: hypargyrium confine
side_effects: Hypargyrium confine can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can lead to respiratory problems, including coughing and shortness of breath. In severe cases, improper use of hypargyrium confine can cause damage to the liver and kidneys, resulting in fatigue, weakness, and swelling. Additionally, some individuals may experience allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and rashes, after coming into contact with the plant.
